name: Fageda d'en Jordà, Santa Margarida, and Croscat Volcanoes Trail
name_original: Volcán de Santa Margarida y Volcán del Croscat - trail run circular por el Parque Natural de la Zona Volcánica de la Garrotxa
description: Explore the Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest and two volcanoes on this moderate 7.6-mile jogging trail in Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park.
region_intro: Jogging around Olot offers a unique experience within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, characterized by its distinctive volcanic terrain and lush forests. The region features numerous extinct volcanoes, including Montolivet and Montsacopa, alongside the enchanting Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest, which grows on an ancient lava flow. The city is also traversed by the River Fluvià, providing diverse riparian landscapes for running. This varied topography creates a dynamic environment for runners of all levels. Best jogging routes around Olot The most popular jogging route is Fageda d'en Jordà, Santa Margarida, and Croscat Volcanoes Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This route explores the iconic beech forest and two prominent volcanic cones. Another top favourite among local runners is Olot Greenway – Fluvià River – Ermita de la Salut loop from Olot, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This route follows a scenic greenway alongside the Fluvià River, offering a less strenuous option. Local runners also love the Plaça Clarà – Plaça Rector Ferrer loop from Olot, a 13.7 miles (22.1 km) trail leading through urban and surrounding natural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Jogging around Olot is defined by volcanic cones, ancient beech forests, and river valleys. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails with elevation changes. The routes in Olot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,600 runners have used komoot to explore Olot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Olot?
A: Olot offers a wide variety of running and jogging routes, with over 120 trails available. These routes cater to all levels, including 3 easy, 79 moderate, and 43 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every runner within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners or families in Olot?
A: Yes, Olot has several easy running trails perfect for beginners or families. The renowned &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955434750/fageda-d-en-jorda-santa-margarida-and-croscat-volcanoes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà, Santa Margarida, and Croscat Volcanoes Trail&lt;/a&gt;, while moderate overall, includes sections through the flat, enchanting Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest, which is known for its stroller-friendly paths. The Carrilet Greenway, a converted railway line, also offers a mostly flat and scenic option for less strenuous jogs.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Olot?
A: Absolutely. Many of Olot's running paths are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10486861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olot Greenway – Fluvià River – Ermita de la Salut loop from Olot&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic moderate loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10487767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà – View to Olot loop from Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; provides another excellent circular option through the volcanic landscape.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while jogging in Olot?
A: Jogging in Olot offers a chance to explore unique natural attractions. You can run through the ancient Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest, which grows atop a lava flow, or ascend extinct volcanoes like Montsacopa for panoramic views. The region also features beautiful riparian landscapes along the River Fluvià. For those interested in waterfalls, highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí dels Murris Pool&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3304941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Cotilla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are accessible near some routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the Olot area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer runs, Olot provides several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Clarà – Plaça Rector Ferrer loop from Olot&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.7-mile (22.1 km) trail that takes you through urban and natural areas. Additionally, the Carrilet Greenway extends from Olot to Girona, offering a significantly longer, mostly flat path ideal for extended runs.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Olot?
A: The running trails in Olot are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 16 reviews. Runners frequently praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the serene beauty of the Fageda d'en Jordà, and the well-maintained network of paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Olot provide stunning scenic viewpoints, especially those that ascend the extinct volcanoes. For instance, jogging to the top of Montsacopa volcano offers panoramic views of Olot and the surrounding Garrotxa Volcanic Zone. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10487767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà – View to Olot loop from Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; also includes sections with great vistas.

Q: Is it possible to access running trails in Olot using public transport?
A: Olot is well-integrated with its natural surroundings, and many trails are accessible directly from the town center. While specific public transport details for each trailhead vary, the city's location within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park means that many routes, including the climb to Montsacopa, can be started without needing a car.

Q: Are there any parks in Olot suitable for shorter jogs or warm-ups?
A: Yes, Olot features several green spaces ideal for shorter jogs or warm-ups. Parc Nou, with its botanical garden, and Pedra Tosca Park, known for its unique landscape design, offer pleasant environments for leisurely runs within the city.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Olot?
A: The Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park offers a pleasant environment for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable. Winter offers crisp air and unique views, though some higher elevation paths might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any routes that combine urban exploration with natural scenery?
A: Yes, Olot seamlessly blends urban and natural environments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Clarà – Plaça Rector Ferrer loop from Olot&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you through both the city's areas and its surrounding natural landscapes. You can also find trails that start right from the town, such as the path leading up to Montsacopa volcano, offering a mix of city views and natural exploration.
tips: La Fageda d'en Jordà is a beautiful beech forest located on a cooled lava flow from a volcano. It is located within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, very close to some of its most impressive volcanoes such as the Croscat Volcano or the Santa Margarida Volcano.

Sant Miquel Sacot is a Romanesque church in La Cot, a scattered center of Santa Pau (La Garrotxa, Catalonia). It is at about 640 masl. It is a Romanesque church rebuilt and extended in the 18th century in neoclassical style. During the 18th century, it belonged to the Monastery of Sant Pere de Besalú. The place belonged to the barony of Santa Pau. It is within the limits of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. It is a building that forms part of the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia. Nearby is the Rectory of Sant Miquel Sacot.

The Santa Margarida volcano, in Catalan Volcà de Santa Margarida, is an extinct volcano in the northeast of Catalonia. It lies between Olot and Santa Pau and, as part of the Garrotxa volcanoes nature reserve, is a popular destination. Its last eruption was about 11,000 years ago.

The Croscat volcano and its clay pots are one of the jewels of the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. It is the second largest volcano in the Iberian Peninsula, and its last eruption was 11,500 years ago.
Its particular shape is due to the fact that for 25 years it was dedicated to the exploitation of clay, for the production of bricks and tennis courts.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Fageda d'en Jordà, Santa Margarida, and Croscat Volcanoes Trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered moderate for jogging, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. While some sections, like the Fageda d'en Jordà, are relatively flat, the route includes ascents and descents around the volcanoes, with some potentially slippery parts. It's best suited for experienced trail runners rather than casual joggers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this route typically takes around 1 hour and 32 minutes to complete. If you plan to walk or combine jogging with walking, especially to enjoy the scenery, allow for a longer duration, as walking can take approximately 4 hours and 20 minutes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain. You'll start in the unique Path Through La Fageda d'en Jordà, a beech forest growing on a lava flow with an undulating relief. Beyond this, expect a mix of hiking paths, footpaths, gravel, unpaved earth, and natural surfaces. There are also short stretches of road and asphalt, with climbs and descents around the volcanoes. Some sections can be a little slippery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail showcases three prominent features of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. You'll experience the unique Path Through La Fageda d'en Jordà, the wide crater of Santa Margarida Volcano with its Romanesque hermitage, and the impressive 'scar' or Gredales of the Croscat Volcano, a result of past quarrying. You'll also pass by the Hermitage of Sant Miquel de Sacot.

faqs.question: Is this a circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a popular circular route, making it convenient for starting and ending at the same point without needing to arrange transportation back.

faqs.question: Where can I park, and is there public transport access?
faqs.answer: As a popular route within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, there are designated parking areas, often near the Fageda d'en Jordà or Santa Margarida Volcano access points. Due to its popularity, especially on weekends, arriving early is recommended to secure a parking spot. For public transport, check local bus services that connect to the park's main access points, though direct access to specific trailheads might be limited.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but it is particularly striking in autumn when the foliage of the Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest changes color. Spring also offers lush greenery. Summers can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winters can be cool, requiring appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Fageda d'en Jordà, Santa Margarida, and Croscat Volcanoes Trail?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always follow park regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park or this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or permits required to access the trails within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, including this route. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes in the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of Itinerary 1 of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park and intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers 100% of the '1 Fageda d'en Jordà - Volcà de Santa Margarida - Volcà del Croscat' trail and significant sections of 'Volta a peu a la Garrotxa' and 'El Camí IT1TR3 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer'.

faqs.question: What makes the Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest so unique?
faqs.answer: The Path Through La Fageda d'en Jordà is an ecological marvel because beech forests typically don't grow at such low altitudes (around 550 meters) or on lava flows. Its unique undulating relief, formed by the ancient lava from the Croscat volcano, creates a distinctive and enchanting landscape.

waypoints.name: Path Through La Fageda d'en Jordà
Hermitage of Sant Miquel de Sacot
